source_timestamp_basis: git commit timestamp of c1b1cfd, the last commit touching the browser scenario ledger
subject: canonical browser editor oracle coverage
claim: The canonical six-scenario editor oracle has no audio-playback scenario or audio predicate; its only full-editor scenario proves a persisted move, diagnostics, DOM state, and text-selection policy.
impact: A green bun run drive verify result cannot rule out complete preview silence.
implication: Audio playback needs its own approved full-editor scenario rather than being inferred from the general browser oracle.
notes: blind_spot: canonical completion oracle does not exercise Play/Pause or inspect window.__veanAudio

source_timestamp_basis: git commit timestamp of 18bd17d, the last commit touching the component test
subject: mute and volume component coverage
claim: The component audio test mounts Transport with local React state and a mocked app API; it does not mount FootageStage or AudioGraph.
evidence_quote: const [volume, setVolume] = useState(0.75); const [muted, setMuted] = useState(false); ... <Transport ... />
impact: The test proves the menu label and slider state change, not that the mixer receives those values or produces sound.
implication: A broken prop handoff, zero master gain, suspended context, failed buffer load, or disconnected graph all pass this test.

source_timestamp_basis: git commit timestamp of e4dca61, the last commit touching the Chrome media spec
subject: codec and fixture signal assurance
claim: The Chrome audio cells fetch fixture bytes into a fresh standalone AudioContext, call decodeAudioData, and compute RMS directly from the decoded AudioBuffer.
evidence_quote: const context = new AudioContext(); ... const audio = await context.decodeAudioData(bytes); ... Math.sqrt(sum / data.length)
impact: This strongly proves PCM, AAC, and MP3 fixtures decode to non-silent PCM in Chromium.
implication: It does not prove the editor resolves those clips, fetches them through AudioGraph, schedules sources, applies non-zero gains, or reaches an output destination.
notes: The media.chrome.audio predicate is decode assurance, not audible editor playback assurance.

source_timestamp_basis: git commit timestamp of e4dca61, the last commit touching the Chrome media spec
subject: silent-audio adversarial control boundary
claim: The silent-audio and swapped-channel negative controls mutate the already-collected standalone RMS result, then the verifier rejects that synthetic result; they do not mutate the product AudioGraph or its routing.
evidence_quote: if (live && control === "silent-audio") live.pcm.rms = live.pcm.rms.map(() => 0); ... if (... rms.every((value) => value < 0.001)) throw
impact: The control establishes sensitivity of the codec-result checker but cannot demonstrate sensitivity to a disconnected master gain, muted editor state, or zero scheduled product sources.
implication: A product-path negative control should disconnect/zero the real mixer signal and make the new editor-output assertion fail.
notes: blind_spot: the named silent-audio control can pass even when the editor path is already silent

source_timestamp_basis: git commit timestamp of 074d262, the last commit touching product-media.ts
subject: product audio play-state coverage
claim: The product-path lifecycle presses Space and waits only for __veanMediaState().audio.playing === true; although ProductState includes contextState, scheduledClips, and bufferedResources, this assertion does not check any of them.
impact: The product lifecycle passes before proving context resume, decode completion, a scheduled source, or a buffered audio resource.
implication: At minimum the test should require contextState=running, bufferedResources>0, and scheduledClips>0 for an explicit audio-track fixture.
notes: biggest_surprise_candidate: the richest product state is available but the gate uses only its weakest boolean

source_timestamp_basis: git commit timestamp of a71c5ca, the last commit touching AudioGraph
subject: playing flag semantics
claim: AudioGraph.play sets playing=true before awaiting resume and scheduling, while getStats reports playing independently of scheduledClips and bufferedResources.
evidence_quote: async play(...) { this.playing = true; await this.resume(); this.schedule(resolved); }
impact: The product-media wait in audio-harness-005 can succeed even if resume fails or every decode returns null.
implication: playing must not be treated as evidence of audible output; scheduling and measured signal need independent assertions.
notes: The implementation explicitly catches resume failure and loadBuffer decode/fetch failures, allowing a silent state without a rejected play promise.

source_timestamp_basis: git commit timestamp of e207b9b, the last commit touching the performance sample
source_title: Chrome product playback A/V skew sample
subject: audio-context liveness and A/V clock coverage
claim: The performance sample does exercise an explicit audio track and requires AudioGraph playing=true plus contextState=running, then compares playhead frames with AudioContext.currentTime.
impact: This proves autoplay unlock/context liveness and shared-clock advancement in the production viewer.
implication: It still permits scheduledClips=0, bufferedResources=0, master gain 0, mute=true, or a disconnected output because it samples no audio amplitude and does not assert those available counts.
notes: blind_spot: clock alignment is not signal propagation

source_timestamp_basis: filesystem mtime of the installed Playwright core bundle
subject: headless Chromium output muting
claim: Every current Playwright Chromium launch in the browser/media suites uses headless:true without overriding defaults, and Playwright adds --mute-audio in headless mode.
impact: No existing headless run can serve as evidence that sound was physically emitted by Chromium, even when the Web Audio graph is running correctly.
implication: Keep automated proof speaker-safe and assert the pre-device PCM signal; do not describe headless playback as audible. If a physical-output test is intentionally added, it must explicitly remove --mute-audio and capture a controlled loopback device.
notes: biggest_surprise: the canonical browser runtime is explicitly muted by its automation framework

source_timestamp_basis: git commit timestamp of b1ae880, the last commit touching the WKWebView media spec
subject: native WebKit audio coverage
claim: The native WKWebView audio matrix also creates a standalone AudioContext, decodes fixture bytes, computes buffer RMS, and closes the context; it never resumes, creates a source, starts playback, connects the editor mixer, or inspects output routing.
evidence_quote: const audioContext = new AudioContext(); ... decodeAudioData ... getChannelData ... await audioContext.close()
impact: H07 proves native WebKit codec decode of non-silent fixtures but not editor playback in the actual app.
implication: The native provider needs a product-editor playback cell against a timeline with an explicit audio clip and the same pre-device signal oracle used in Chromium.
notes: blind_spot: native decode matrix is not a native editor audio-path test

source_timestamp_basis: git commit timestamp of 5e608db, the last commit touching the native editor spec
source_title: H05 actual Tauri WKWebView editor test
subject: native full-editor scenario coverage
claim: The only actual native full-editor scenario selects a clip, invokes split, saves, and screenshots; it never activates Play, opens the audio controls, or queries __veanAudio.
evidence_quote: it("persists one real split through the existing editor and Save path" ... edit("split" ... save.click()
impact: A fully silent native app passes the strongest actual-WKWebView editor gate.
implication: Native shell identity and save persistence evidence cannot be reused as audio evidence; add a dedicated native playback assertion.

source_timestamp_basis: git commit timestamp of a71c5ca plus test-design analysis at collection time
subject: smallest reliable reproduction and assertion
claim: A deterministic regression test should load an explicit audio-track timeline using the existing asymmetric PCM fixture, perform a real user Play gesture, and measure non-zero PCM after the product master gain but before the physical device; current __veanAudio stats should simultaneously require contextState=running, bufferedResources>=1, scheduledClips>=1, muted=false, volume>0, and the intended sink/default route.
evidence_quote: AudioGraphStats currently exposes contextState, scheduledClips, bufferedResources, and playing, but not master volume, mute, sink, decode errors, or output level.
impact: This separates document resolution, decode, scheduling, mixer controls, and actual signal propagation while remaining headless and not disturbing the user's speakers.
implication: Implement a harness-only analyser/AudioWorklet tap on the master-gain output (or init-script interception of the master connection to a MediaStreamAudioDestination), collect several hundred milliseconds, and require RMS/peak above a fixture-derived threshold. Negative controls must zero/disconnect the real master path and fail; mute must drive RMS near zero and unmute must restore it. Run the same bridge through H07 WKWebView. Physical device emission remains a separate OS-loopback/manual boundary.
notes: blocker: current bridge cannot observe master volume, muted state, sink success/failure, decode errors, or PCM amplitude; a small harness instrumentation surface is required for a deterministic assertion
